## Frequently asked questions

[What is an envelope?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-bc2283069d90d9ccea77)

An envelope is the process of sending a document to one or more recipients for electronic signing. It involves preparing the document by adding fillable fields and then sending the document to recipients to sign electronically.

[Who is a "sender"?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-8417e7169d90d9ccea77)

A “sender” is anyone who has a BoldSign account and can send documents out for signature. Anyone that just receives a document for signing is classified as a “recipient” and does not need a BoldSign account or a license.

[How do I estimate my cost?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ddac1bc69d90d9ccea77)

Use the calculator link.

[Will my signers need to pay or need an account for signing?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3dad4e069d90d9ccea77)

No, your signers do not need to pay or register with us to sign the documents that you send.

[Will my subscriptions be automatically renewed?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ecc498569d90d9ccea77)

Yes, your subscriptions will be automatically renewed at the end of the billing cycle. However, you can easily cancel anytime by logging into your account.

[Are there any limitations during the free 30-day trial?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-af2712069d90d9ccea77)

All trial accounts will have access to the enterprise-level features, so everyone will be able to evaluate all the available features. However, there are some usage restrictions that apply:

- Maximum number of senders is restricted to 25.
- Maximum number of documents that can be signed is restricted to 250.
- Maximum number of documents that can be signed using bulk links is restricted to 250.
- Maximum number of API documents is restricted to 250.

[Are BoldSign’s eSignatures legally compliant?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3b895a169d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign is legally compliant with popular U.S. (ESIGN) and international (eIDAS) eSignature laws. We record all the major activity that happens during the signing process and provide a downloadable copy of the complete audit trail report. The final document is also digitally signed so that any potential tampering in the future can be immediately detected.

[Does BoldSign offer special pricing for non-profit organizations?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-7227f4869d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign provides a specially designed eSignature solution for non-profit organizations. Our Growth Plan is completely free, and we provide a 50% discount for Business and Premium plans. Discover more on our [non-profit page](https://boldsign.com/solutions/electronic-signature-for-non-profit-organizations/ "https://boldsign.com/solutions/electronic-signature-for-non-profit-organizations/").

[What is the Pay As You Go option in BoldSign?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-5d0c2eb69d90d9ccea77)

Our [Pay-As-You-Go](https://support.boldsign.com/kb/article/21241/pay-as-you-go-for-boldsign) (PAYG) model works with Business plans and above. You get all core features in your subscription, and PAYG applies only when you exceed your limits or use add-ons like SMS delivery, WhatsApp notifications, bulk sending, identity verification, and more.

[Is there a free way to request an e-signature?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-07b7de469d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign offers a free Essential plan that allows individuals and micro-businesses to request up to 25 envelopes per month with no cost, including features like audit trails, signer authentication and automated reminders.

[How much does BoldSign cost?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-68e27d969d90d9ccea77)

BoldSign offers a free plan(Essential), paid plans start at $5/month(Growth), Business and Premium include advanced features for teams, and Enterprise plans with API access start at $30/month for additional enterprise-level capabilities.

[What is an envelope?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-bc2283069d90d9ce54c0)

An envelope is the process of sending a document to one or more recipients for electronic signing. It involves preparing the document by adding fillable fields and then sending the document to recipients to sign electronically.

[Who is a user?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-8417e7169d90d9ce54c0)

In the API plan, a user is anyone responsible for configuring the BoldSign account for integration purposes. This includes tasks like generating API keys, creating OAuth apps, monitoring the developer console, and managing webhook configurations.

[How does API pricing work?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3dad4e069d90d9ce54c0)

**Free Sandbox:** This plan is for testing our APIs. You can integrate our APIs into your application for free. However, the documents created will be watermarked as “This is a test document” and automatically deleted after 14 days.

**Enterprise API:** The Enterprise API pricing starts from $30/month, includes 40 envelopes. Once you’ve used the 40 envelopes, a pay-as-you-go plan will get started, costing $0.75 per envelope.

**Custom Plan:** We also provide customized pricing options to fit your unique needs.

Please refer to this link for more information about API pricing: [BoldSign API Pricing](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=api)

[How are envelopes counted?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ecc498569d90d9ce54c0)

You will be charged per envelope sent for signature. You can add multiple documents within a single envelope, and they will be considered as one envelope.

[Can I test BoldSign's API?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-af2712069d90d9ce54c0)

Yes, you can test BoldSign’s API with the free Sandbox plan. Remember, documents created with this plan will be watermarked, and deleted after 14 days.

[Will my documents be secure?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3b895a169d90d9ce54c0)

Yes, documents created with BoldSign are entirely secure. Your data is protected so that even our staff cannot access it for troubleshooting purposes. Please refer to this link for more information: [BoldSign security policy](https://boldsign.com/security-policy/).

[Which plan is best for me and my team?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-091cb6869d90d9ce54c0)

Check out [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cj5SWTOJdWM) to compare our different paid plans and select the one that best fits your needs.

# Rate limit

BoldSign has rate limits to balance loads on the system. Both API keys and OAuth API calls follow the same rate limit system. In a live or production environment, you can make up to 2000 requests per hour per account. In sandbox mode, this limit is set at 50 requests per hour per account.

The rate limits are introduced for the account level, not for the OAuth app or user level.

Once the limit is reached, responses with a status of 429 "Too Many Requests" and corresponding messages will be returned. You can track your rate limit usage in the API response headers.

![Rate limit](https://developers.boldsign.com/static/images/api-overview/ratelimit.webp)

APIs have become an integral part of modern software development, enabling seamless communication among different software systems. However, as APIs grow in popularity and usage, developers face the challenge of managing the load on servers to ensure optimal performance. One of the critical mechanisms employed to address this challenge is API rate limiting. This blog post explores the concept of rate limiting, its importance, best practices for managing it, and how BoldSign APIs implement rate limits to ensure fair usage.

## What is Rate Limiting?

Rate limiting is a technique used to control the number of requests a user or an application can make to an API within a specific timeframe. It acts as a gatekeeper to prevent abuse, protect server resources, and ensure the equitable distribution of resources among all users. When the rate limit is exceeded, the server typically returns a status code of 429 Too Many Requests, indicating that the client must slow down its request rate.

![API rate limit exceeded](https://boldsign.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/api-rate-limit-exceeded-1.png) API rate limit exceeded

## Why Is Rate Limiting Used?

Rate limiting serves several critical purposes:

- **Preventing Abuse:** Rate limits protect APIs from abuse, such as DoS (denial of service) attacks, where an attacker may flood the server with requests to overload it.
- **Ensuring Fair Usage:** By enforcing rate limits, API providers ensure that no single user consumes more than their fair share of resources, allowing equitable access to all users.
- **Optimizing Server Performance:** Limiting the rate of requests helps maintain optimal server performance by preventing spikes in traffic that could lead to slow response times or server crashes.
- **Protecting Service Quality:** Rate limits protect the back-end systems from being overwhelmed by too many requests, which could degrade the quality of service for all users.

## Understanding BoldSign API Rate Limits

BoldSign implements [rate limits](https://developers.boldsign.com/api-overview/rate-limit?region=us) to balance system loads and ensure smooth operations. Here are the key aspects of BoldSign’s rate-limiting policy:

- **Production Environment:** In a live or production environment, BoldSign allows up to 2,000 requests per hour per account.
- **Sandbox Environment:** In sandbox mode, which is used for testing and development, the rate limit is set to 50 requests per hour per account.
- **Account-Level Limits:** Rate limits are applied at the account level rather than the OAuth app or user level.
- **Response Headers:** The API response headers provide information about the current rate limit status, allowing developers to track usage.

![Response header showing the remaining rate limit](https://boldsign.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/response-header-showing-the-remaining-rate-limit-1.png) Response header showing the remaining rate limit

## Strategies for Managing Rate Limits

- **Understand Rate Limit Specifications:** Familiarize yourself with the specific rate limits imposed by your API provider, such as BoldSign. This includes knowing the difference in limits between production and sandbox environments and understanding how these limits apply to your account.
- **Prioritize Essential API Requests:** Identify which API requests are critical to your application’s functionality and prioritize them. This ensures that crucial operations are not affected when nearing rate limits.
- **Implement Application-Level Rate Limiting:** Design your application to impose its own rate limits before the API provider’s limits are reached. This helps prevent accidental overuse and provides an additional layer of control.
- **Monitor Usage and Optimize Requests:** Continuously monitor your API usage patterns and identify opportunities to reduce unnecessary calls. Use these to optimize your code, combining multiple requests or simplifying operations to stay within limits.
- **Use Webhooks for Real-Time Updates:** Set up [webhooks](https://developers.boldsign.com/webhooks/introduction/?region=us) to receive event-driven notifications from the API. This approach minimizes the need for constant polling, thereby reducing the number of API calls and conserving your rate limit quota. For guidance on configuring webhooks, see [Configure Webhooks](https://developers.boldsign.com/webhooks/verify-webhook-events/?region=us).
- **Consult with Support for Custom Solutions:** If your application requires higher API usage, consult with [BoldSign’s support team](https://support.boldsign.com/agent/tickets/create). We can offer advice or custom solutions tailored to your specific needs, ensuring you can make the most of the API within the rate limits.

## Conclusion

API rate limits are essential for maintaining the performance and reliability of APIs, ensuring fair usage, and protecting against abuse. BoldSign’s rate-limiting policy provides clear guidelines for both production and sandbox environments, allowing developers to plan and scale their API usage accordingly. By adhering to these practices, developers can build applications that handle API rate limits efficiently and avoid disruptions in service.

Implementing these strategies helps in managing rate limits and also contributes to building resilient applications that can grow with your business needs.

###### FAQs

## Frequently asked questions

[What are embedded signatures?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-bc2283069d9124d68961)

Embedded signatures enable you to embed the signature request sending and signing interfaces on your own application. This allows your users to send or sign documents seamlessly within your application, enhancing their overall experience.

[Does the signer need an account for the embedded signing?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-8417e7169d9124d68961)

No, signers don’t need a BoldSign account or pay any fees to sign documents in the embedded signing process.

[What are sender identities?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-3dad4e069d9124d68961)

Sender identities are necessary to send signature requests on behalf of another user. Simply add the intended user as the sender identity, but you will need to get their approval before proceeding. Please visit our [API documentation](https://developers.boldsign.com/sender-identities/create-identity/) for more details.

[Are BoldSign eSignatures secure?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-ecc498569d9124d68961)

At BoldSign, security is our top priority. We ensure the utmost user data and documents protection through end-to-end encryption and adherence to industry regulations like GDPR and SOC 2.

[How BoldSign charges for eSignature API?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-60aa17569d9124d68961)

BoldSign charges for its eSignature API on a per-document basis. It costs $0.75 per document. Please visit our [pricing](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/) page for more information.

[Does BoldSign support embedded signing on mobile?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-c56fad569d9124d68961)

Yes, BoldSign fully supports embedded signing on mobile devices. You can integrate the signing experience directly into your mobile app using BoldSign’s API and SDKs, which are designed to be responsive and developer-friendly.

[How to embed a signature in a document?](https://boldsign.com/embedded-signatures/#collapse-3b2fe4c69d9124d68961)

With BoldSign, upload your document, generate a signing link, and embed it in your app or site using a secure iFrame with just a few lines of code for a seamless signing experience.

## Navigating API Pricing for Developers: A Business Perspective on eSignature Integrations

In the rapidly evolving landscape of digital agreements, developers seeking to integrate eSignature capabilities into their applications face a critical decision: balancing functionality, scalability, and cost. As businesses increasingly prioritize seamless API-driven workflows for automation, compliance, and user experience, platforms like DocuSign and BoldSign emerge as key contenders. This article delves into a neutral comparison of their API pricing models, drawing from official documentation and market observations as of 2025. From startups building lightweight integrations to enterprises handling high-volume transactions, understanding these structures helps developers align costs with project needs without overcommitting resources.

## DocuSign API Pricing: A Tiered Approach for Scalable Integrations

DocuSign, a longstanding leader in electronic signatures, offers a robust Developer API platform tailored for embedding signing workflows into custom applications. Its pricing is structured around annual billing for production use, emphasizing envelope quotas (the number of documents processed) and feature depth. This model suits developers building enterprise-grade solutions where compliance and reliability are paramount.

### Core API Plans and Costs

DocuSign's API plans start with the **Starter** tier at $600 per year, providing around 40 envelopes per month. This entry-level option includes basic API access, OAuth authentication, and SDK support in multiple languages, ideal for small integrations like simple contract automation in web apps.

Moving up, the **Intermediate** plan costs $3,600 annually and unlocks 100 envelopes per month, adding collaborative features such as scheduled sending and comments. Developers integrating into team-oriented tools, like CRM systems, find this useful for handling moderate volumes without immediate scaling pains.

The **Advanced** tier, priced at $5,760 per year, maintains the 100-envelope quota but introduces premium capabilities like Bulk Send API, PowerForms for dynamic forms, and Connect Webhooks for real-time notifications. This is geared toward high-automation scenarios, such as SaaS platforms processing bulk user agreements.

For larger needs, the **Enterprise** plan is custom-priced, often exceeding $10,000 annually based on volume and seats, offering unlimited customization, SSO, and advanced governance. All plans include usage monitoring via an API dashboard, but overages incur metered fees—typically $0.10–$0.50 per extra envelope, depending on the tier.

### Strengths and Considerations

From a business viewpoint, DocuSign's pricing reflects its market dominance, with strong global compliance (e.g., ESIGN Act in the US, eIDAS in Europe) and extensive documentation. However, the seat-based licensing in underlying eSignature plans can indirectly impact API costs for multi-user integrations, and add-ons like SMS delivery or identity verification add per-use fees (e.g., $0.05–$0.20 per message). Developers should note that automation sends, including API-triggered ones, are capped—around 100 per user per year in standard setups—potentially requiring upgrades for high-throughput apps.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1927_Docusign_simple_compose_01kb2gzcxjf90s2d849a4yzkes_f4a40eeee0.png)

## BoldSign API Pricing: Affordable Entry for Agile Development

BoldSign, developed by Zoho as part of its ecosystem, positions itself as a developer-friendly alternative with a focus on simplicity and cost efficiency. Its API pricing is more straightforward and volume-based, appealing to SMBs and indie developers who want robust eSignature features without the premium markup of established giants. Launched to compete in the mid-market, BoldSign emphasizes seamless integration with Zoho's suite, making it a natural fit for businesses already in that ecosystem.

### Core API Plans and Costs

BoldSign's API access is bundled into its subscription tiers, starting with a free developer sandbox for testing unlimited envelopes in non-production environments—a boon for prototyping. For live use, the **Basic** plan is $10 per user per month (billed annually at $120), including API access with up to 50 envelopes per month and core features like embedded signing and templates.

The **Standard** tier, at $20 per user per month ($240 annually), supports 200 envelopes monthly and adds advanced API endpoints for bulk operations, webhooks, and conditional fields. This level suits developers building customer-facing apps, such as onboarding portals.

Higher volumes fall under the **Premium** plan ($40 per user per month, $480 annually), offering unlimited envelopes for API use (with fair-use policies) and extras like custom branding, payment collection, and priority support. Enterprise customizations start around $1,000 monthly, scaled by API calls and compliance needs.

Unlike DocuSign, BoldSign avoids strict envelope caps in premium tiers, charging overages at a flat $0.05 per envelope. Add-ons, such as SMS authentication, are $0.02 per delivery, keeping ancillary costs low.

### Strengths and Considerations

BoldSign's pricing model promotes accessibility, with no separate developer sandbox fees and easy scaling via Zoho's pay-as-you-grow philosophy. It complies with major standards like UETA/ESIGN and GDPR, though its global reach is more limited compared to DocuSign. Businesses observe that while feature parity is high (e.g., both support OAuth and SDKs), BoldSign's lower entry point—often 50-70% cheaper for mid-tier use—makes it attractive for cost-sensitive projects. Drawbacks include less mature ecosystem integrations outside Zoho apps and potentially slower support for complex customizations.

## Head-to-Head: DocuSign vs. BoldSign API Pricing Breakdown

At the heart of developer decisions lies direct cost comparison. For a small team integrating basic signing (40-100 envelopes/month), DocuSign's Starter/Intermediate starts at $600-$3,600 yearly, versus BoldSign's Basic/Standard at $120-$240— a stark 80-90% savings with BoldSign. As volumes grow to 100+ envelopes with bulk features, DocuSign's Advanced jumps to $5,760, while BoldSign's Premium caps at $480 for unlimited use, highlighting BoldSign's edge in scalability without tier locks.

Feature-wise, both offer embedded signing and webhooks, but DocuSign excels in enterprise governance (e.g., advanced audit trails), justifying its premium for regulated industries like finance. BoldSign counters with faster setup times and Zoho synergies, reducing total ownership costs. Metered add-ons tilt toward BoldSign's affordability ($0.05/envelope vs. DocuSign's variable $0.10+), though DocuSign's quotas enforce predictability for budgeting. In business terms, DocuSign suits high-stakes, global deployments; BoldSign fits agile, budget-driven innovation.

This comparison occupies the bulk of developer evaluations, as API pricing directly impacts ROI—DocuSign for proven reliability, BoldSign for lean efficiency.

## Broader Market Landscape: Key Competitors in eSignature APIs

To contextualize, let's examine other players. Adobe Sign integrates deeply with Adobe's creative and document tools, offering API plans starting at $10/user/month for basics (50 envelopes), scaling to enterprise custom (unlimited) with strong PDF handling. HelloSign (now Dropbox Sign) provides a free tier for developers (3 envelopes/month), with paid API access at $25/user/month (100 envelopes), emphasizing simplicity for non-technical teams.

This table underscores a diverse market: DocuSign leads in maturity, while alternatives like BoldSign and eSignGlobal prioritize value.

### Adobe Sign: Enterprise Depth with Creative Flair

Adobe Sign's API is renowned for its synergy with Acrobat and Creative Cloud, enabling developers to embed signing into PDF workflows. Pricing mirrors DocuSign's tiered structure but leverages Adobe's volume discounts for bundled services.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1941_Adobe_simple_compose_01kb2httnmftqttna4mq0d3mrb_3366684d27.png)

### eSignGlobal: Global Compliance with APAC Focus

eSignGlobal stands out for its compliance across 100 mainstream countries, with a particular edge in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region. APAC's eSignature landscape is fragmented, featuring high standards and strict regulations that demand ecosystem-integrated solutions—unlike the more framework-based ESIGN/eIDAS in the US/Europe. Here, platforms must enable deep hardware/API-level docking with government digital identities (G2B), a technical hurdle far beyond email verification or self-declaration models common in the West.

eSignGlobal's API is bundled into its Professional plan (contact sales, starting around $299/year for Essentials), supporting unlimited users and 100+ documents without seat fees. This yields high cost-effectiveness—Essentials at about $24.9/month allows 100 documents, access code verification, and seamless integrations like Hong Kong's iAM Smart or Singapore's Singpass. It's competitively priced against DocuSign and Adobe Sign, often 20-40% lower, while expanding globally to challenge incumbents in Europe and the Americas.

FAQs

What are the main differences in API pricing structures between DocuSign and BoldSign for developers?

DocuSign employs a tiered pricing model based on envelopes, with API access included in plans starting at around $25 per user per month for basic features, scaling up for higher volumes and advanced API calls, often requiring additional fees for excessive usage. BoldSign, on the other hand, offers a more straightforward per-envelope pricing for API integrations, starting at $0.10 per envelope with volume discounts, and includes unlimited API calls in its developer plans without per-user licensing. For developers in Asia facing compliance needs, eSignGlobal provides a compliant alternative with flexible API pricing tailored to regional regulations.

How does DocuSign's API pricing compare to BoldSign's in terms of cost for high-volume developer workflows?

For high-volume workflows, DocuSign's pricing can become complex, with costs escalating based on envelope limits and add-ons, potentially reaching $0.50 or more per envelope in enterprise tiers, plus overage fees for API exceeding plan limits. BoldSign maintains lower costs at scale, with pricing as low as $0.05 per envelope for large volumes and no hidden API fees, making it more predictable for developers. Developers prioritizing Asia-specific compliance may find eSignGlobal's API model more suitable, offering competitive per-transaction rates aligned with local standards.

Are there any free or trial options for DocuSign and BoldSign APIs, and how do they impact pricing decisions?

Both DocuSign and BoldSign provide developer sandboxes with free API access for testing, but production usage incurs costs immediately upon going live. DocuSign's trial includes limited envelopes, transitioning to paid plans, while BoldSign offers a generous free tier for up to 5 envelopes monthly before scaling to paid API usage. For developers evaluating options, especially in Asia where compliance is key, eSignGlobal's trial API access with full compliance features can serve as a robust alternative without immediate pricing commitments.
